title: Tragacantha oreades
intro: Tragacantha oreades, also known as Mountain Combretum or Combretum oreades, is a plant species belonging to the Combretaceae family, characterized by its small, yellowish flowers and woody stems. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. Bioactive constituents of Tragacantha oreades include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to address respiratory issues and boost the immune system.
uses: Tragacantha oreades, also known as mountain salsify, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots and leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and wounds.
